latest.title: Trading Update
latest.content:
17 Jul 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 7204M
Itaconix PLC
17 July 2026
For release: 17 July 2026
Itaconix PLC
H1 2026 Trading Update
FY 2026
revenue to be ahead of expectations
Itaconix plc (AIM: ITX) (OTCQB: ITXXF),
a leading innovator in high-performance plant-based specialty polymers used in everyday consumer products such as homecare detergents,
announces a strong trading update for the six months ending 30 June 2026.
Itaconix delivered a record first half with unaudited revenues at $8.3 million
representing +72% growth vs H1 2025 ($4.8 million), and +46% growth vs H2 2025 ($5.7 million),
with growth achieved across all major product segments. Gross profit margin for H1 2026 is expected to be in line with FY 2025 at 36%, following robust management of raw materials and supply chains.
Performance Ingredients, primarily serving the dish detergent market, performed well in both EMEA and North America. Growth was driven by strong reorder volumes from existing customers, a new unit-dose dish detergent customer in EMEA, and a new unit-dose laundry detergent customer in North America.
SPARX
™
Formulated Solutions experienced growth due to the increased use of SPARX™ formulations in North America for solid unit-dose dish and laundry detergents. Revenues are generated from the sale of other key ingredients that contract manufacturers need to produce SPARX™ formulations.
The Company is advancing longer-term revenue opportunities with its BIO*Asterix® specialty monomers and binders in high-value paints, and its new BioVail™ plant nutrition ingredient in crop production.
Outlook
We are pleased with progress across the business and at this stage of the financial year. Whilst re-order rates are yet to be firmly established, the Board now expects revenue for the year to 31 December 2026 to be no less than $14.8 million, ahead of current market expectations1. The Board continues to expect to deliver a small positive EBITDA for FY 2026, reflecting strategic investments in headcount and product development to drive near-term growth and advance longer-term revenue potential.
Further information on H1 2026 results will be provided in the 2026 interim results which are expected to be released on or about 8 September 2026.
1
Prior to the release of this announcement, the Board understands that analyst forecasts for FY 2026 indicate revenue of $13.3 million and EBITDA of $0.3 million.
Certain information contained in this announcement would have been deemed inside information as stipulated under the UK version of the EU Market Abuse Regulation (2014/596) which is part of UK law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018, as amended and supplemented from time to time, until the release of this announcement.
About Itaconix
Itaconix plc produces and sells plant-based specialty ingredients that improve the safety, performance and environmental profile of everyday consumer and industrial products. The Company's proprietary polymer technology platform is enabling new generations of products in home care, hygiene, and emerging areas in paints and agriculture.

latest.title: Customer Agreement Update
latest.content:
14 Jul 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1558M
Itaconix PLC
14 July 2026
For release on Reach: 14 July 2026
Itaconix plc
("Itaconix", the "Company" or the "Group")
Customer Agreement for Compact Plastic-Free Dish Detergent Tablet
Itaconix plc (AIM: ITX) (OTCQB: ITXXF), a leading innovator in high-performance plant-based specialty polymers used in everyday consumer products such as homecare detergents,
announces its first customer supply agreement for its 8-gram plastic-free dish detergent tablets.
Under its SPARX Formulated Solutions business, Itaconix will supply dish detergent tablets to a North American purpose-driven brand using the pilot-scale tableting capacity at its North American Innovation Center. The tablet was developed around the unique performance advantages of Itaconix® TSI® polymers and the Bonals tablet press. The new tablet was introduced
at the American Cleaning Institute's Innovation Showcase in February 2026 and is available to brand managers and retail customers on the Company's deeperclean.com ecommerce site.
This supply agreement is an important milestone in the commercial introduction of the Company's patented plastic-free detergent tablets in North America. The new tablets are expected to be available on retail shelves by the end of 2026.
John R. Shaw, CEO of Itaconix, commented:
"Our plant-based ingredients are leading a new generation of plastic-free solid unit-dose detergents in Europe and North America because they are safe for the environment and offer unique value with their multi-functional performance. We are already gaining volumes in dish and laundry detergent tablets in North America. Supply agreements to a select number of purpose-driven brands will allow us to push further innovation onto the market and progress efforts with North American contract detergent manufacturers to produce our proprietary tablets on high-speed tablet presses."
- Ends -

About Itaconix
Itaconix uses its proprietary plant-based polymer technology platform to produce and sell specialty ingredients that improve the safety, performance, and sustainability of consumer products. The Company's current ingredients are enabling and leading new generations of products in detergents, hygiene, and hair care. Itaconix's products contribute to the global low carbon economy.
https://itaconix.com/
About Bonals Technologies
Bonals Technologies is a tableting technology provider serving customers worldwide. With 1000+ machines currently producing, the company developed strong partnerships with
leading detergent tablet brands and contract manufacturers in Europe and Asia thanks to
innovative and ecological solutions dedicated to homecare, laundry care and chemicals.
https://www.bonals.com/

latest.title: Agreement Extended with Bonals Technologies
latest.content:
8 Jul 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4050L
Itaconix PLC
08 July 2026
For release on Reach: 07:00, 8 July 2026
Itaconix plc
("Itaconix", the "Company" or the "Group")
Agreement Extended with Bonals Technologies
Itaconix plc (AIM: ITX) (OTCQB: ITXXF), a leading innovator in high-performance plant-based specialty polymers used in everyday consumer products, such as homecare detergents,
announces an extension of its collaboration with Bonals Technologies ("Bonals") through to December 2028 under the Company's SPARX Formulated Solutions business. The program seeks to speed the introduction of safer, better performing, cost effective, and more sustainable unit dose detergent tablets in the United States and Canada.
As announced in November 2024, Itaconix and Bonals signed an agreement to collaborate on developing new generations of detergents that leverage the synergistic benefits of Itaconix formulations produced on Bonals tableting equipment. In 2025 the two companies worked together to install a pilot-scale tablet press in Itaconix's North American Innovation Center to develop its patented 8-gram plastic-free dish detergent tablet.
This collaboration resulted in Itaconix introducing a new dish detergent tablet at the American Cleaning Institute's Innovation Showcase in February 2026 which is generating high levels of interest from purpose-driven brands in North America. The new tablet is available on the Company's deeperclean.com ecommerce site, and was also featured in a recent Chemical and Engineering News article on the new potential and growth in solid unit-dose detergents.
As demand for the Company's Performance Ingredient grows to existing North American contract tablet makers, the extended collaboration will assist Itaconix in progressing efforts with contract manufacturers to install high-speed Bonals tablet presses that produce more compact and faster dissolving tablets based on the synergy between their compression cycle and Itaconix detergent polymers. The Company plans to use its pilot-scale press to gain commercial traction and build volumes for its proprietary tablet with a select number of brands.
Olivier Desmarescaux, CEO of Bonals Technologies, commented:
"We are excited about the innovative detergent tablets that Itaconix has developed on our tableting press. I believe our collaboration with Itaconix is keeping us at the forefront of introducing high-performing, plastic-free tablets in North America that are both competitively priced and better for the environment. We expect continued success working together on new opportunities in North America."
John R. Shaw, CEO of Itaconix, commented:
"Our work with Bonals and purpose-driven brands in our North American Innovation Center is demonstrating the sizable opportunity for us to replicate our success of European tablets in North America. We are particularly excited about the positive response to our patented 8-gram plastic-free dish detergent tablet produced on a Bonals tablet press. The extension of our collaboration with Bonals offers us further opportunities for leading a new generation of solid unit-dose detergents."
- Ends -

latest.title: Mello 2026 Presentation and Update on Trading
latest.content:
2 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 5423G
Itaconix PLC
02 June 2026
Embargoed for 07.00, 2 June
2026
ITACONIX PLC
("Itaconix" or the "Company")
Mello 2026 Conference Presentation and Update on Trading
Extending Plant-Based Innovation into New Markets
Itaconix plc (AIM: ITX) (OTCQB: ITXXF), a leading innovator in high-performance plant-based specialty polymers, will present today at 13.55 BST at Mello 2026 Investor Conference.
Following our most successful year to date in FY2025, the Company reports that trading in the current year has continued in line with the Board's expectations. The management expects H1 FY2026 will be another record half. The Board remains confident in the Company's strategy, customer pipeline and long-term growth opportunities.
Our core Itaconix® Performance Ingredients business continues to build momentum in consumer detergent applications for scale inhibition and odour neutralisation, supported by increasing customer adoption of safer, higher-performing and more sustainable ingredients. We continue to see encouraging activity levels from major customers and are closely tracking reorder rates from new customers to assess expected growth in 2026.
The Middle Eastern conflicts are creating disruptions in the production and supply of fossil-based specialty chemicals that compete against our ingredients. The Company continues to monitor logistics and transportation costs within our plant-based supply chain which have not experienced significant disruption or unfavourable cost implications, and our overall gross profit margins remain steady. Current and potential customers are recognising the stability in the supply and pricing of our ingredients, which we believe will translate into new revenue opportunities.
Driving Growth Through Innovation
In May 2026, the Company announced the launch of its new deeperclean.com marketing initiative aimed at North American consumer detergent brand owners. The flagship product on deeperclean.com is the 8 grams plant-based dishwashing detergent tablet that Itaconix introduced at the American Cleaning Institute's Innovation Showcase in February this year. The ecommerce site allows brand and formulation managers to readily receive and evaluate the novel performance and marketing claims available with our ingredients. We expect to conduct consumer evaluations to test, refine and substantiate the performance of our formulations.
In May 2026, the Company also announced the development of a new class of paints under the Company's BIO*Asterix® programme. During this next stage of development, the Company will work with an established specialty paint company to evaluate paint products using these new esters and latexes.
In June 2026, the Company announced evaluation agricultural trials of new potential applications for our current ingredients in crop production under the tradename BioVail™ GRZ™ 200L. Results of these trials are expected at after harvest. This work will require assessment over multiple growing seasons.
- Ends -
Itaconix plc
John R. Shaw / Laura Denner

company_30s.what_they_do: Itaconix Corporation develops and sells natural polymer materials and modified polymer‑based products for use in coatings, adhesives and packaging. Their core lines include natural polymers, modified polymers, self‑adhesive plates and sheets, and a range of plant‑based paints.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company is positioned in the growing clean‑tech and sustainability space. Demand for plant‑derived coatings and eco‑friendly packaging is rising as regulations tighten and consumers shift toward greener products. Itaconix’s proprietary polymer chemistry gives it a niche advantage over commodity plastic suppliers.
company_30s.current_state: The business is still in a high‑growth phase but is operating on a tight cash burn. Recent RNS suggest the company is working to ramp up production, launch new marketing initiatives and secure additional financing. Its share price is trading at a very low level relative to its long‑term valuation, implying a steep recovery potential if fundamentals improve.

rns_narrative: The company has been steadily moving through a product‑development cycle: crop‑production trials, a new plant‑based paint line, and a fresh marketing push. These events signal a shift from R&D to commercialization.
Governance updates—AGM results, director share‑holdings, and option grants—show that senior management is actively investing in the business, which often signals confidence.

Investor‑focused communications, such as the June 2 presentation and the preliminary results release, indicate that the company is preparing for a more transparent financial picture in the near future.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but watchful – a small group of insiders is quietly buying, while the wider community stays silent.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of posts from insiders praise the director’s share purchases and the upcoming plant expansion. They see the company as a hidden gem in the green‑tech niche.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Some traders note that the buying volume is modest and that the stock remains extremely thinly traded, suggesting a risk of liquidity squeezes or further dilution.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone is steady: no dramatic swings. The community is largely indifferent, with occasional bullish chatter but no sustained optimism.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat—no public search interest is building, which is a classic sign that a surprise catalyst could still hit.

near_term_catalysts: Q3 earnings release: a positive earnings surprise would validate the product pipeline and likely lift the stock.
Announcement of a new manufacturing facility or partnership with a major packaging firm would signal scale‑up.
Significant share‑buyback or dividend declaration would demonstrate cash flow strength.
A spike in trading volume above the 50‑day average—especially on a day the price touches a key support level—would confirm that the market is starting to price in the upside.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a clear break above the 20‑day moving average on a volume surge, a 10‑day volume spike that exceeds the 30‑day average, and any forward guidance that lifts the company’s revenue outlook.
expect_next.timing_regime: If the stock is still in the early capitulation phase, expect a slow, patient recovery. A breakout at the next support level could be the start of a 3‑4 month run. The APEX score is high, indicating a tight compression that could explode once the catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company’s next earnings report shows a 20‑30% revenue lift and the share price climbs to 50% of its pre‑crash level, the upside could be 200‑300% over a 6‑month horizon. The contrarian model shows that deep‑crash signals often generate the largest rebounds, and the insider buying suggests a long‑term conviction.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that the product pipeline fails to scale or that the company runs out of cash before it can complete the next phase of production. A sudden liquidity crunch or a regulatory setback could push the price back to zero.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is roughly 4‑to‑1. The downside is capped at the current price level (the stock is near zero), so the asymmetry favors a bullish stance if the catalyst arrives.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the 20‑day MA with a volume spike of at least 2× the 50‑day average. Size the position so that a 5‑day pullback to the 20‑day MA would trigger a stop‑loss. Target the next support level at roughly 50% of the pre‑crash peak.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see either a clear earnings upside or a volume surge that breaks the 20‑day MA. Watch the 50‑day MA as a dynamic support; if the price dips below it on high volume, consider exiting or tightening the stop.
playbook.if_passing: None identified—there is no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risk, so the setup remains viable.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep crash with no public chatter, insider buying and a pipeline of green‑tech products points to a high‑reward, low‑downside play if the next earnings release confirms growth.
